items.claim: Fatima Klein uses stance changes effectively to close distance for clinches or takedowns.
items.quote: And she uses those stance changes to close the distance if she wants to get to the clinch and take the fight to the ground

items.claim: Fatima Klein is mobile enough to alleviate pressure by changing stances and using footwork.
items.quote: she's mobile enough that she can alleviate some pressure if it's brought upon her by changing stances and using footwork.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez relies on single strikes but barrels forward with heavy punches if he gets an opponent moving backward.
items.quote: what I learned from Mitch Ramirez is you're going to get a lot of single strikes, but if he can get you going backwards, that's when he's really going to start to barrel forward and throw heavy punches.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez has a tendency to overextend his head past his front foot.
items.quote: at times he does get his head ahead of his front foot

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez uses level changes and takedowns to finish sequences when he gets an opponent to the fence.
items.quote: if he does and he can get you to the fence, he will clean it up by changing levels and shooting takedowns.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez was sidelined for over a year due to damage sustained from low kicks in his loss to Thiago Moises.
items.quote: he lost to Thiago Moises by low kicks in the third round and that put him out for over a year.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez's striking style is linear and lacks lateral movement or check punches.
items.quote: he doesn't really strike laterally. He doesn't really throw check punches. Everything's pretty linear.
